title: Wireless Channel Utilization and Interference
dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:wireless:rf`. via custom scripted input polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/wireless/rf-profile and per-AP detail from Assurance` (deploy per docs/guides/catalyst-center.md § Custom Scripted Inputs). Default poll interval: 900s (15m). Volume: ~1 event per AP per poll. RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `apName` (string), `band` (string), `channel` (integer), `channelUtilization` (integer 0-100), `interferencePercentage` (integer 0-100), `txPowerLevel` (integer), `noiseFloor` (integer).
app: `Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7538) + custom scripted input for wireless RF
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:wireless:rf" | stats avg(channelUtilization) as avg_util avg(interferencePercentage) as avg_interference by apName, band, channel | eval util_status=case(avg_util>80,"Overloaded",avg_util>60,"High",1==1,"Normal") | sort -avg_util
description: Monitors wireless channel utilization and interference levels across access points and frequency bands to identify RF contention and capacity issues.
value: High channel utilization and interference directly cause poor wireless performance. Monitoring these metrics enables proactive RF optimization and AP density planning.
implementation:
Channel utilization and interference data requires polling the Catalyst Center wireless-specific APIs.

API endpoints:
• `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/device-detail?searchBy=macAddress&identifier=<ap_mac>` — per-AP detail including RF metrics
• `GET /dna/intent/api/v2/data/device-health` — aggregated wireless metrics

Create a custom scripted input:

```ini
[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA_catalyst_wireless/bin/collect_wireless_rf.py]
interval = 900
sourcetype = cisco:dnac:wireless:rf
index = catalyst
disabled = 0
```

The script should poll AP detail for each access point and extract: `apName`, `band` (2.4GHz, 5GHz, 6GHz), `channel`, `channelUtilization`, `interferencePercentage`, `txPower`, `clientCount`. Use `POST /dna/system/api/v1/auth/token` to authenticate. Prefer discovering AP MACs from the TA’s `devicehealth` (UC-5.13.1) or inventory API to drive the per-AP loop. Recommended sourcetype: `cisco:dnac:wireless:rf`.
visualization: Heatmap or table of `avg_util` by `apName` and `band`, bar chart of top overloaded channels, line chart of utilization over time.
knownFalsePositives:
**High utilization expected in high-density venues during peak hours.** Stadiums, lecture halls, conference centers, and retail spaces experience very high channel utilization during events or peak business hours. This is expected and does not indicate a problem. Distinguish by checking whether the high utilization correlates with known event schedules or business hours at high-density sites. Suppress by maintaining a `catalyst_site_thresholds` lookup with per-site utilization baselines and alerting only on sustained deviation from the site-specific baseline.

**Non-Wi-Fi interference sources creating elevated channel utilization.** Bluetooth devices, microwave ovens, cordless phones, and industrial equipment can create RF interference that Catalyst Center reports as channel utilization. Distinguish by checking whether the interference is localized to specific physical areas or channels, and whether it correlates with non-Wi-Fi device activity. Suppress by tuning the interference threshold per AP and flagging persistent interference sources for physical site survey.

**DFS channel event causing temporary utilization spike.** When radar is detected on a DFS channel, the AP must vacate the channel and move clients to a new channel, causing a temporary utilization spike on the target channel. Distinguish by checking whether the utilization spike coincides with a DFS radar event on a neighboring channel. Suppress by allowing a 30-minute stabilization window after a DFS event.

**AP radio reset or reboot causing momentary utilization data gap followed by spike.** When an AP radio restarts, clients reconnect simultaneously, creating a burst of activity. Distinguish by checking whether a data gap immediately precedes the utilization spike. No suppression needed — track as an AP health event.

references.title: Catalyst Center Wireless RF Profile API — Cisco DevNet

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) with the **networkhealth** or wireless RF modular input enabled, writing to `index=catalyst`.
• Catalyst Center **2.3.5+** with Assurance wireless analytics enabled.
• APs managed by Catalyst Center with RF monitoring active.
• RBAC: service account with **NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE**.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Channel utilization and interference data is collected from the Assurance wireless analytics via `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/network-health` or specific wireless health endpoints. The data is polled at the TA's configured interval.

Key fields:
• `channelUtilization`: percentage of airtime used on the AP's radio channel (0-100%). Includes both data traffic and non-Wi-Fi interference.
• `interferenceScore` or `interferenceHealth`: Assurance score for RF interference (0-100, higher is better).
• `noiseFloor`: background noise level (dBm) — higher noise floor indicates more interference.
• `apName`: AP hostname.
• `frequency` or `band`: 2.4 GHz, 5 GHz, or 6 GHz.
• `channel`: specific radio channel number.
• `siteId`: site UUID.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst (sourcetype="cisco:dnac:networkhealth" OR sourcetype="cisco:dnac:devicehealth") deviceType="*AP*"
| stats avg(channelUtilization) as avg_utilization avg(interferenceScore) as avg_interference by apName, siteId, frequency, channel
| eval util_status=case(avg_utilization > 80, "Critical", avg_utilization > 60, "High", avg_utilization > 40, "Moderate", 1==1, "Normal")
| eval interference_status=case(avg_interference < 30, "Severe Interference", avg_interference < 50, "Moderate Interference", 1==1, "Normal")
| where util_status!="Normal" OR interference_status!="Normal"
| sort avg_utilization desc
```

Understanding this SPL:
• **`deviceType="*AP*"`**: Filters to access point devices — only APs have channel utilization and interference metrics.
• **`avg(channelUtilization) ... by apName, frequency, channel`**: Averages utilization per AP per radio per channel. High utilization on a specific channel suggests co-channel interference or heavy traffic.
• **`util_status`**: >80% channel utilization typically causes client performance degradation. >60% may be acceptable in high-density venues but warrants monitoring.
• **`interference_status`**: Assurance interference scores below 30 indicate severe RF interference affecting client connectivity.

Step 3 — Validate
• **UI comparison:** Open Catalyst Center > Assurance > Health > Network Health > [site] > RF. Compare the channel utilization and interference metrics with the Splunk results.
• **AP 360 check:** Select a specific AP in Catalyst Center > Assurance > Device 360. Compare the RF dashboard with the Splunk per-AP results.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Dashboard:** Channel utilization heatmap by AP and channel, interference score distribution, and a table of APs with critical utilization or severe interference.
• **Alert:** Trigger when `avg_utilization > 80` sustained for 30+ minutes for any AP — indicates a capacity or interference problem.
• **RF optimization:** High utilization on specific channels may be resolved by RRM (Radio Resource Management) tuning, channel width adjustment, or AP density changes.

Step 5 — Troubleshoot
• **High utilization in high-density venues is expected:** Use per-site thresholds from a `catalyst_site_thresholds` lookup.
• **DFS channel events causing utilization spikes:** Radar detection on DFS channels forces AP channel changes, temporarily spiking utilization on the target channel. Check for DFS events in the AP logs.
• **Interference from non-Wi-Fi devices:** Bluetooth, microwaves, and cordless phones cause interference on 2.4 GHz. Consider moving to 5 GHz or 6 GHz bands.
• **No wireless RF events arriving:** RF monitoring requires a custom scripted input; verify deployment and check `index=_internal` for API error or endpoint timeout messages.
• **NULL or zero values in utilization:** an AP that just rebooted resets its counters; empty values indicate the radio is administratively disabled.
• **Fewer records than expected:** check that APs are discovered in Catalyst Center Inventory; if credential access is denied, check the service account role.
grandmaExplanation: We check how crowded the wireless radio channels are at each access point, finding areas where too many devices or too much interference is slowing things down.
